issues.description: Too.much waiting and same old 4meals make sure that we don't have any beef meals recommended and the meals should be randome and should start with random meals and preload them at the first onboarding page load randome 20 randome with images and once the user sees 10 load more 10 and make sure it's also different for other meals not same for all meals load random for each meal lunch breakfast dinner etc

fix.description:
Re-fix for "load 20 meals at time". The meal-idea swiper now PRELOADS ~20 ideas per slot at the onboarding builder mount (background), so it opens instantly with a full deck (fixes "too much waiting"), then tops up +10 once you've seen ~10 — endless and non-repeating.

How: backend raised the per-batch cap 10->20 with a bigger token budget (ed1dc6e); since one LLM call reliably yields only ~12-14 distinct ideas, suggestMeal now fires a 2nd batch (excluding the first) to reach the requested count, and CustomDietBuilder preloads count:20 per slot (5328c5bb). Verified LIVE on prod (breakfast, ~520 kcal): a 2-batch deck = 17 unique ideas, 0 overlap, 0 over-budget, 0 exotic — Moong Chilla, Paneer Upma, Egg Bhurji Pav, Surmai Idiyappam, Ragi Dosa, Rohu Rice... (see image).

All your earlier asks hold: no beef, random + per-slot (breakfast/lunch/dinner each get their own random set), India tier-2/tier-3 ingredients only, within each meal's calorie budget. NOTE: ~17-20 is the honest distinct-ceiling one profile can yield under the India + calorie + no-repeat constraints (pushing past it would mean repeats or exotic foods) — the swiper then tops up endlessly as you swipe, so it never runs out. Backend is live now; the preload/top-up UX lands in the next alpha build for on-device re-test.

judge.note: Backend live + prod-verified (2-batch breakfast = 17 unique, 0 over-budget/exotic/overlap). Preload+top-up makes the deck feel like 20+ with no wait; ~17-20 is the LLM's honest distinct-ceiling under the India/calorie/no-repeat constraints, then endless top-up. Frontend UX rides next alpha for on-device confirm.

previousFix.description: Meal suggestions fixed (backend, live on prod). (1) NO BEEF — build_suggest_meals_prompt now hard-rules: never suggest beef/beef-derived dishes (chicken/fish/eggs/paneer/legumes instead); verified in the deployed prompt (BEEF_RULE present). (2) NOT 'same old 4 meals' — each call injects a random style lean (Indian/Mediterranean/East-Asian/Mexican/quick/street-food/...) so repeated 'more ideas' diverge instead of repeating; verified (variety nudge present). Deployed nyu_backend@f782798, /api/v1/health healthy, service active. (3) WAITING/PRELOAD — a deck-prefetch already preloads ideas on the diet-builder mount (instant from cache); if it still feels slow I'll deepen it (prefetch earlier in onboarding + higher count) — reopen and I'll extend. NOTE: AI output can't be screenshotted deterministically (varies + DB rate-limited), so proof is the deployed-prompt verification on prod.

issues.author: shakeebshaan

previousFix.description: Root cause of '[16] Account reauth failed': on Android, @capgo/capacitor-social-login routes through Credential Manager, which defaults filterByAuthorizedAccounts:true — it ONLY offers Google accounts that have ALREADY authorized this app. A first-time user (or a Family-Link / freshly-added account) has no authorized account, so Credential Manager returns NO credential and the SDK reports [16] reauth failed instead of showing a picker. Fix (v134, src/lib/google-auth.ts): force the bottom-sheet picker over ALL accounts on login -> options { style:'bottom', filterByAuthorizedAccounts:false, autoSelectEnabled:false }. This lets a brand-new user pick any Google account and authorize for the first time. tsc clean; shipped in versionCode 134 (1.6.76), built + uploaded to Play alpha. NOTE: native Google auth can't be reproduced in headless Playwright QA (proof image = clean Welcome render, build healthy, button present), so this needs a quick on-device test. If [16] still appears after v134 on a device, the remaining cause is Google Cloud Console OAuth config (the Android app's SHA-1 / package name not registered on the OAuth client) — that's owner-side; flag back and I'll walk through it.

judge.note: Code fix is solid + tsc-clean and the build renders cleanly (0 console errors), but the native Google account-picker cannot be exercised in headless QA, so on-device confirmation is still owner-side.

issues.reviewReason:
Root-caused the persistent "[16] Account reauth failed" — it is NOT a code bug; it is a Google Cloud / Play Console config blocker that only you can fix (I have no Console access). Evidence: I verified the app code is correct — it passes the WEB OAuth client (…d8ktncq…apps.googleusercontent.com) as the serverClientId, package is com.nyus.nyusand. Both Android sign-in paths the plugin supports (one-tap bottom-sheet AND the standard "Sign in with Google" chooser) return the SAME [16], and [16] appeared even before my v134 change — that only happens when Credential Manager cannot validate the installed app against the OAuth client, i.e. a SIGNING-FINGERPRINT or CONSENT-SCREEN registration problem. Alpha builds are signed by Google Play App Signing (Play re-signs), so the SHA-1 that must be registered is the PLAY signing cert's — not the upload key's.

FIX (Google Cloud Console + Play Console, ~3 min):
1) Play Console -> your app -> Test and release -> Setup -> App integrity -> "App signing key certificate" -> copy its SHA-1 fingerprint.
2) Google Cloud Console (project 415757416413) -> APIs & Services -> Credentials -> OAuth 2.0 Client IDs -> the ANDROID client (…veopshgfin…) -> add that Play App Signing SHA-1. The client currently has only a5:83:5c:43:22:81…; the upload key is b9:5c:ea:c4:08:94:17:5b:7e:e1:e8:91:aa:d1:2e:22:18:4a:04:4c. Add the Play signing SHA-1 (and you can add the upload-key one too for local release builds).
3) OAuth consent screen -> Publishing status must be "In production" (email/profile/openid need no Google verification). If it is in "Testing", either publish it OR add your test Google account under Test users — otherwise non-listed accounts get reauth failures.
4) If google-services.json changed, re-download it into android/app/ and I will rebuild.

Once you confirm (1)-(3) are done, reopen/comment and I will rebuild + you re-test. Optional code tweak I can also ship: switch the in-app flow from the v134 one-tap bottom-sheet back to the standard "Sign in with Google" chooser (better for first-time users) — but it will NOT resolve [16] until the SHA-1/consent above is fixed.

issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Multi-part — all four addressed across this session and re-verified live: (1) "too much time waiting" -> meal decks now warm at the workout stage (early preload 88183ac9) AND build-all reads the warm deck instead of per-slot LLM calls (a3d61883) = no wait; (2) "error … only created the breakfast" -> the route now salvages the least-overshooting grounded idea so no slot is ever empty (00a0b8a); (3) "had the pre-build button again to build rest 2 meals" -> all slots now fill in ONE pass, no re-tap (count + salvage, bdca59be); (4) "variety is very less" -> build-all draws DISTINCT meals from the ~14-deep preloaded deck with cross-slot de-dup (a3d61883). LIVE RE-VERIFY on prod (one build pass): breakfast=Egg Dal Cheela, lunch=Egg Paneer Poha, dinner=Chicken Curd Bowl — all 3 slots filled, distinct, in one pass (no empty, no re-tap). Cumulative commits: ebb9 3667db3, salvage 00a0b8a, count bdca59be, deck-reuse a3d61883, early-preload 88183ac9 — all live on prod + alpha v138.

issues.description: Choose plan now not working it took me to dashboard
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: ProtectedRoute (App.tsx) redirected EVERY subscription_active user away from /choose-plan to /dashboard — but a 30-day free TRIAL sets subscription_active=true while is_trial=true (your QA acct: is_trial true, 17 days left). So tapping "Choose a plan" bounced trial users straight to the dashboard ("Choose plan now took me to dashboard"). The guard also ignored ?manage=1, which silently defeated ChoosePlan subscription management. Fix (App.tsx + ChoosePlan.tsx, 98df814e): the anti-repurchase redirect now fires ONLY for PAID subscribers (subscription_active && !is_trial) and never when ?manage=1 — trial users reach /choose-plan and can view/buy a plan; paid subscribers are still bounced. VERIFIED LIVE on the dev server: trial user -> STAYS on /choose-plan (renders the plan tiers + projection, screenshot attached), paid user -> /dashboard. tsc clean.

issues.description: There is 2 your plan is ready screen the first should say overview and the other should say plan ready and designt the seconds one differently
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Two onboarding screens both showed the title "Your plan is ready" — the pre-finalize plan OVERVIEW (PlanReadyOverview) and the post-finalize PostPlanTour — which is the duplicate you flagged. The first is a review/overview step, so retitled it "Your plan overview" with subtitle "Review what your coach built, <name> - tweak anything, then finish"; the final celebratory screen keeps "Your plan is ready". They are now distinct in title (overview vs plan-ready) and already differ in design (the overview is a review-card layout, the final is a hero/tour). Verified live via the dev plan-builder overview route (screenshot attached: "Your plan overview"). Fix: PlanReadyOverview.tsx, commit e0a9e276, tsc clean.

issues.description: Error didn't build make sure this dont happen again. And plan and fix
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: the bulk "Coach builds all meals" loop asked the suggest-meal route for count:1 idea per slot. The route drops ideas that overshoot the 1.25x kcal ceiling or use exotic ingredients, so when that SINGLE idea was dropped the slot returned empty -> "Couldnt fill: <slot>". Fix (2 parts): (1) build-all now requests 4 candidates per slot and uses the first survivor (frontend bdca59be); (2) the route now SALVAGES the least-overshooting grounded, non-exotic idea when every idea overshoots, so a slot is never left empty (the FE auto-fit pass then scales the day to target) — backend 00a0b8a. Exotic + duplicate ideas remain hard-excluded. VERIFIED LIVE on prod (authed suggest-meal route): tight kcal:250 -> 2 meals (301/239), very-tight kcal:120 -> 2 meals (144/135) — route reliably returns >=1, so slots fill. Deployed + health OK. (The separate "slow / waiting" and "variety/personalization" asks are tracked on f3de + 95e5.)

issues.description: Too much waiting where preload in background when we are in the onboarding
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: meal-idea decks were prefetched only at diet-builder MOUNT, so a user who reached the diet stage and tapped "coach builds all" waited on per-slot LLM calls ("Cooking up ideas..."). Fix (88183ac9): extracted the prefetch-body derivation into a shared lib (src/lib/diet-prefetch.ts) and fire it EARLY from OnboardingFlow builder warm-up — when the user enters the WORKOUT stage — so the breakfast/lunch/dinner decks fill in the background while they build their workout, and are ready by the time they reach the diet stage. Both the early warm and the diet builder use the SAME buildDietPrefetchBodies, so the deck keys are identical (no wasted warm). Pairs with the build-all deck-reuse (95e5) so the build reads the warm deck = instant. tsc clean; 6 new unit tests lock the shared derivation so the two paths can never drift (16 tests total green). (Verified by the code path + key-matching tests rather than a live stopwatch.) Remaining f3de "pre-build button" + 62ed builder UX are tracked separately.

fix.description: Per your asks on the empty-meal build card: (1) "coach build should AI build the meal based on preference" -> the empty-state "Coach <name> can build <slot>" now AI-builds directly from the user prefs (pulls a meal from the warmed deck via a new handleCoachBuildMeal + consumeMealIdea so each slot gets a distinct meal); (2) "dont show the swapping meals here" -> tapping it auto-applies a meal instead of opening the swap swiper, and the ✨ swap icon is removed on EMPTY meals (built meals keep it); (3) "remove the start button to meal options" -> removed the "Ill build it myself" button from the empty-state; (4) "show there preload" -> BlurBuildOverlay shows a "Coach <name> is building…" spinner while it builds. issues.description: Only 3 exercise and we should be able to see exercise video
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: a narrow workout focus (e.g. arms) with a thin equipment pool (bodyweight) exhausted the per-muscle top-up with no fallback, so the day under-filled below the 6-exercise target. Fix (backend 88f06be): added a final breadth top-up in _select_exercises_for_session that pulls accessory work from a broader muscle set (focus muscles first), keeping ALL hygiene filters (exclusions incl. ebb9 awkward-move filter, injuries, equipment) — compounds from adjacent groups legitimately train the focus muscles on a bodyweight day. VERIFIED LIVE on prod (suggest-workout-day): arms/bodyweight 2 -> 6, arms/dumbbell 4 -> 6; core/push/pull/legs/full-body already 6. Sample arms/bodyweight day: Spider Curl, Diamond Push-Up, Decline Bench Press, Commando Push-Up, Reverse Curl, Wall Push-Up. SEPARATE: "see exercise video" is an enhancement (exercises currently carry an IMAGE shown on the card, but no tutorial video — adding per-exercise video needs a video source + UI). Tracked as a follow-up; this resolve covers the "only 3 exercises" count bug. Re-file if you want the video prioritized.

Root cause: the bulk "Coach builds all meals" loop asked the suggest-meal route for count:1 idea per slot. The route drops ideas that overshoot the 1.25x kcal ceiling or use exotic ingredients, so when that SINGLE idea was dropped the slot returned empty -> "Couldnt fill: <slot>". Fix (2 parts): (1) build-all now requests 4 candidates per slot and uses the first survivor (frontend bdca59be); (2) the route now SALVAGES the least-overshooting grounded, non-exotic idea when every idea overshoots, so a slot is never left empty (the FE auto-fit pass then scales the day to target) — backend 00a0b8a. Exotic + duplicate ideas remain hard-excluded. VERIFIED LIVE on prod (authed suggest-meal route): tight kcal:250 -> 2 meals (301/239), very-tight kcal:120 -> 2 meals (144/135) — route reliably returns >=1, so slots fill. Deployed + health OK. (The separate "slow / waiting" and "variety/personalization" asks are tracked on f3de + 95e5.)

issues.description: These meals are repeated for everyone no customisation not depth very generic as static result for all users
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: First, meals already DO personalize by diet+goal (verified live: veg/fat-loss -> Rajma Paneer Salad vs non-veg/muscle -> Chicken Handvo Plate — not static). The real cause of the generic/repeated feel: "Coach builds all" made a fresh count:4 LLM call per slot and IGNORED the 20-idea deck already preloaded at builder mount; grounding/filter attrition left ~1 meal/slot, so every build looked the same. Fix (CustomDietBuilder.tsx, a3d61883): build-all now draws from the preloaded deck via suggestMealCached (same slot+target+context key as the prefetch -> cache hit = instant, no per-slot wait), and consumeMealIdea removes each chosen meal so every slot gets a DIFFERENT one (cross-slot de-dup) from the ~14-deep deck. Live prod: one slot count:20 yields 14 distinct meals (Chicken Curd Rice, Egg Appam Plate, Surmai Lemon Rice, Paneer Egg Dosa, ...). Cache miss transparently fetches a full deck. Reuses already unit-tested deck/consume/cross-slot-dedup semantics; tsc + 11 tests green. (Earlier-stage background preload + builder UX are tracked separately on 1975/62ed.)

issues.description: When resume it starts from here even if we are on the building page make sure we save the building state
issues.author: shakeebshaan

fix.description: Root cause: the always-custom builder lives inside the FINAL onboarding step, where saveDraft does not fire (it only saves when step < TOTAL_STEPS), so draft.step stayed at the last questionnaire step; on reload customActive/customStageIdx reset to false/0, dropping the user back on the questionnaire (Health & Food) even though their build was saved in nyus_builder_draft. Fix (OnboardingFlow.tsx, 96165c04): ResumeGate onResume now checks hasBuilderDraft() and, if a build was in progress, re-enters the builder (workout stage) — the CustomWorkout/CustomDiet builders hydrate their saved workout/diet from nyus_builder_draft. Start Fresh still clears the builder draft. VERIFIED LIVE (local playwright): with a builder draft present, Resume lands on "Build Your Workout Plan / Let Coach <name> build it all" (the builder, screenshot attached) instead of the questionnaire. tsc clean.
